description: federal city and former capital of Russia
description_wiki: Saint Petersburg, formerly known as Petrograd (Петроград) and later Leningrad (Ленинград), is the second-largest city in Russia, after Moscow, the nation's capital.

description: Russian-American musician (1877–1956)
description_wiki: Isabelle Vengerova (born Izabella Afanasyevna Vengerova; 1 March [O.S. 17 February] 1877 – 7 February 1956) was a Russian and American pianist and music teacher.

aliases: N. A. Rimsky-Korsakov Saint Petersburg State Conservatory
Saint Petersburg State Conservatory named after N.A. Rimsky-Korsakov
description: music school in Saint Petersburg
description_wiki: The N. A. Rimsky-Korsakov Saint Petersburg State Conservatory (Russian: Санкт-Петербургская государственная консерватория имени Н. А. Римского-Корсакова) (formerly known as the Petrograd Conservatory and Leningrad Conservatory) is a school of music in Saint Petersburg, Russia.

description: Russian composer, music teacher and conductor (1865-1936)
description_wiki:
Alexander Konstantinovich Glazunov (10 August [O.S. 29 July] 1865 – 21 March 1936
) was a Russian composer, music teacher, and conductor of the late Russian Romantic period.

description: global war originating in Europe, 1914–1918
description_wiki: World War I, or the First World War (28 July 1914 – 11 November 1918), also known as The Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies (or Entente) and the Central Powers.

description: capital and largest city of Ukraine
description_wiki: Kyiv, also Kiev, is the capital and most populous city of Ukraine.

description: city in Crimea, Ukraine
description_wiki: Yalta (Russian and Ukrainian: Ялта) is a resort city on the south coast of the Crimean Peninsula surrounded by the Black Sea.

description: largest city in Turkey
description_wiki: Istanbul is the largest city in Turkey, constituting the country's economic, cultural, and historical center.

description: country in Southeast Europe
description_wiki: Bulgaria, officially the Republic of Bulgaria, is a country in Southeast Europe.

description: capital and most populous city in France
description_wiki: Paris is the capital and largest city of France, with an estimated city population of 2.04 million in an area of 105.4 km2 (40.7 sq mi), and a metropolitan population of 13.2 million as of January 2026.

description: Russian and American conductor, composer, and double-bassist (1874–1951)
description_wiki: Serge Koussevitzky (born Sergey Aleksandrovich Kusevitsky; Russian: Сергей Александрович Кусевицкий, IPA: [sʲɪrˈɡʲej ɐlʲɪkˈsandrəvʲɪtɕ kʊsʲɪˈvʲitskʲɪj];  26 July [O.S. 14 July] 1874 – 4 June 1951) was a Russian and American conductor, composer, and double-bassist, known for his long tenure as music director of the Boston Symphony Orchestra from 1924 to 1949.

description: ballet music by Igor Stravinsky, composed for the ballet of the same title by Vaslav Nijinsky
description_wiki: The Rite of Spring (French: Le Sacre du printemps) is a ballet and orchestral concert work by the Russian composer Igor Stravinsky.

description: country located primarily in North America
description_wiki: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America.

description: American music school in Rochester, New York, U.S.
description_wiki: The Eastman School of Music is the music school of the University of Rochester, a private research university in Rochester, New York, United States.

description: county seat of Monroe County, New York, United States
description_wiki: Rochester is a city in and the county seat of Monroe County, New York, United States.

description: American historian (1903-1995)
description_wiki: Paul George Vincent O'Shaughnessy Horgan (August 1, 1903 – March 8, 1995) was an American writer of historical fiction and non-fiction who mainly wrote about the Southwestern United States.

description: capital and largest city of Massachusetts, United States
description_wiki: Boston () is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Massachusetts.

description: American composer, music theorist, pianist, teacher, publisher, and impresario (1897-1965)
description_wiki: Henry Dixon Cowell (; March 11, 1897 – December 10, 1965) was an American composer, writer, pianist, publisher, teacher, and the husband of ethnomusicologist Sidney Robertson Cowell.

description: American modernist composer (1874–1954)
description_wiki: Charles Edward Ives (; October 20, 1874 – May 19, 1954) was an American modernist composer, actuary and businessman.

description: composition for orchestra by American composer Charles Ives
description_wiki: The Three Places in New England (Orchestral Set No.

description: Austrian-Jewish American composer (1874-1951)
description_wiki: Arnold Schoenberg or Schönberg (13 September 1874 – 13 July 1951) was an Austrian and American avant-garde composer, music theorist, teacher and writer.

description: open-air theatre in Hollywood, Los Angeles, California, USA
description_wiki: The Hollywood Bowl is an amphitheatre and public park in the Hollywood Hills of Los Angeles, California.

description: Sixth edition of Music Since 1900
book by Nicolas Slonimsky
description_wiki: The Lexicon of Musical Invective is an American musicological work by Nicolas Slonimsky.

description: continent located mainly in the southwestern quadrant of the Earth
description_wiki: South America is a continent entirely in the Western Hemisphere and mostly in the Southern Hemisphere, with a considerably smaller portion in the Northern Hemisphere.

aliases: the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics
description: former country in Eurasia (1922–1991)
description_wiki: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991.

entity: Thesaurus of Scales and Melodic Patterns
surface_forms: Thesaurus of Scales and Melodic Patterns

entity: International Cyclopedia of Music and Musicians
surface_forms: International Cyclopedia of Music and Musicians
description: 1949 edition of encyclopedia
Book by Theodore Baker
description_wiki: Baker's Biographical Dictionary of Musicians is a major reference work in the field of music, originally compiled by Theodore Baker, PhD, and published in 1900 by G. Schirmer, Inc.
